content.content: Captain Mr. Krishna Kaji Thapa, Coordinator, Garuddal Battalion
Administrative Officer Mr. Dil Krishna Shrestha, Member, District Administration Office
Police Inspector Mr. Shriram Timalsina, Member, District Police Office
APF Inspector Mr. Jayanta K.C., Member, Armed Police Force Nepal No. 18 Gulm
Senior Investigation Assistant Mr. Prashant Dotel, Member, National Investigation District Office

Considering reports of prisoners escaping from various jails nationwide, reliable security for the Charikot Jail in Dolakha shall be managed by the Nepalese Army (Garuddal Battalion, Charikot Barracks) and other security units.

Due to the situation following the nationwide Gen Z protests, a curfew is issued effective from 5:00 PM on 2082/05/24 until further notice, under Section 6(a) of the Local Administration Act, 1971. Assembly and movement are prohibited within the following boundaries:
East: Trindhara on the way to Bhimeshwar Temple
West: Simpani
North: Bhimeshwar Municipality on the way to Pakhatole
South: Old Bazaar

In all other areas of Dolakha district outside the mentioned boundaries, a Prohibitory Order (Nishedhagya) remains in effect until further notice, prohibiting any form of assembly, procession, or meeting under Section 6(3a) of the Local Administration Act, 1971.
The implementation of previous District Security Committee decisions is proceeding effectively, and the overall situation in the district is normal.

Reviewed the district's status in light of today's nationwide protests. As of 20:00 PM, the situation is normal except for candle lighting at Dharke (Prithvi Highway) and Emale Chowk (Dhadingbesi). As protests are possible within the district, all security agencies shall strengthen security arrangements and information exchange.
Considering security sensitivities, the following security personnel shall be deployed to ensure the protection of government offices and key locations.
Cordon 1: The road between the Livestock Service Office and the Chamber of Commerce and Industry. Responsibility: APF Dependent Gulm Dhading, 1 Section.
Cordon 2: Campus Chowk (in front of Dhading Hospital). Responsibility: APF Dependent Gulm Dhading, 1 Section.
Cordon 3: The inner road in front of the Office of Industry and Commerce. Responsibility: District Police Office Dhading, 7 personnel under the command of a Junior Police Officer.

Cordon 4: The inner road between the Armed Forest Guard Security Camp and Tundikhel (for the security of the helipad as well). Responsibility: District Police Office Dhading, 7 personnel under the command of a Junior Police Officer.

Regular patrolling of the aforementioned designated Cordons shall be conducted by the Armed Police Force, while patrolling of sensitive areas other than the designated Cordons shall be conducted by the Nepal Police.

In the event of an adverse situation regarding peace and security, the Nepalese Army (Shree Bhairav Battalion) shall be mobilized as per the orders of the Chief District Officer in accordance with the Local Administration Act, 1971.

Since 2082/05/23, protests by the 'Gen Z' group in Kathmandu Valley and various cities across the country have resulted in loss of life and property. As the same group conducted activities such as peaceful protests and candle lighting in this district today, a reliable security arrangement shall be made through mutual coordination among security agencies, focusing on potential future protests or demonstrations.

Protests by the Gen Z group in the district headquarters Dhadingbesi and various market areas along the Prithvi Highway, including chanting slogans, have remained peaceful thus far (19:30 PM) without any loss of life or property.

content: Prisoners and detainees at the Dhading District Jail (Male Wing) have demanded immediate release and have been observed attempting arson and vandalism inside the facility. It has been found that prisoners whose permanent homes are in this district have been calling relatives in Dhadingbesi, requesting to be freed through jailbreaks similar to incidents at Nakkhu Jail. Additionally, some youths have been seen moving around the market area with sharp weapons like khukuris and swords. Assessing this critical situation, the Dhadingbesi area of Neelakantha Municipality Ward No. 3 (bounded by Siran Bazar Chowk to Creek Resort in the East, Hospital Chowk in the West, the road to District Administration in the North, and Arun Khola in the South) is declared a Riot-Affected Area per Section 6(b)(1) of the Local Administration Act, 1971. The Nepalese Army (Shree Bhairav Battalion, Gajuri Barracks) is requested to take action per Section 6(b)(1)(i) of the same act. While a 2:10 PM meeting previously decided to start a curfew at 5:00 PM, considering the security sensitivity of the riot-affected area, a curfew is now ordered from 2:20 PM to 5:00 PM under Section 6(a)(1) of the same act. Security agencies are directed to take action per Sections 6(a)(3) and (4).

content.content: Discussed and reviewed the incidents in the district resulting from the situation created by Gen Z protests in Kathmandu and other cities on 2082/05/23 and 24. The situation in the district is currently moving toward normalcy.

In the context of the country's adverse situation and the ongoing challenge of undesirable activities at night, a curfew order is issued per Section 6(a) of the Local Administration Act, 1971. Movement, assembly, processions, demonstrations, mass meetings, or picketing are prohibited in the following areas of Neelakantha Municipality, Dhading, from 7:00 PM on 2082/05/26 until 6:00 AM on 2082/05/27.

content: District headquarters Dhadingbesi (Neelakantha Municipality): East: Izara Chautara; West: Dhadingbesi-Salyantar Road; North: Thopal Khola; South: Arun Khola.

content.content: Due to potential threats to prison security and overall peace in the district following the Gen Z protests on 2082/05/23 and 24, a curfew was in effect from 5:00 PM on 2082/05/24. As the situation is gradually normalizing, for the convenience of the general public, the curfew is lifted from 5:00 PM to 7:00 PM today (2082/05/26). However, a Prohibitory Order (Nishedhagya) shall remain in effect district-wide during this period.

During the period of the prohibitory order, essential goods such as milk, fruits, vegetables, eggs, meat products, agricultural produce, and medicines shall be allowed uninterrupted transport and movement in coordination with security agencies.
Reviewed the overall peace and security situation of Kathmandu district. The situation is currently normal; security arrangements shall be kept efficient and coordinated in the coming days.

Discussed sensitive activities that may be conducted by various individuals or organizations in Kathmandu on 2082/05/23. Considering security sensitivity, potential activities shall be closely monitored to prevent any undesirable actions through coordinated security arrangements.

To prevent today's demonstrations by the Gen Z group and other parties from turning violent, an integrated security plan approved by the District Security Committee meeting on 2082/05/22 is in place. All officers and personnel on duty shall be briefed on the effective implementation of this security plan.
As there may be significant presence of children in today's demonstrations organized by the Gen Z group, extreme caution must be exercised if force is required for crowd control.

Reviewed the overall peace and security situation of Kathmandu district. While the situation is currently normal, in light of potential sensitive activities by various youth groups regarding the social media ban, security coordination shall be strengthened to ensure error-free security management and prevent undesirable incidents.

Considering the chaotic situation including confrontations, clashes, and riots involving protesters from various youth groups regarding the social media ban on 2082/05/23, it is recommended to the Chief District Officer to issue a curfew order per Section 6(a) of the Local Administration Act, 1971. This shall be effective from 12:30 PM to 10:00 PM on 2082/05/23 in the following areas.
From Baneshwor Chowk west via Everest Hotel up to Bijulibazar Bridge,
From Baneshwor Chowk east via Minbhawan and Shantinagar up to Tinkune Chowk,
From Baneshwor Chowk north via Eyeplex Mall up to Ratna Rajya Secondary School,
From Baneshwor Chowk south via Shankhamul slope up to Shankhamul Bridge.

As unruly activities during protests in the Baneshwor area on 2082/05/23 have disrupted peace and security, a request is made to the Nepalese Army (Narasingh Dal Battalion, Singha Durbar Barracks) to provide necessary assistance in implementing the order issued by the Chief District Officer per Section 6(2) of the Local Administration Act, 1971.

Considering the chaotic situation including clashes and riots during the protests regarding the social media ban on 2082/05/23, it is recommended to the Chief District Officer to issue a curfew order per Section 6(a) of the Local Administration Act, 1971, for the designated areas from 1:00 PM to 10:00 PM on 2082/05/23.
Shital Niwas (President's Office) Maharajgunj Area:
North-East: Up to the intersection in front of the National Police Academy,
East-South: 100 meters south from the turn leading to Samakhushi,
South-West: From the turn leading to Samakhushi up to the Samakhushi River.
Harit Griha (Vice President's Office) Lainchaur Area:
From Keshar Mahal Chowk through Lainchaur turn up to the Malla Hotel boundary.
Narayanhiti Palace Museum Area:
From Keshar Mahal Chowk through the Narayanhiti Palace Museum South Gate up to Jai Nepal turn,
From the Narayanhiti Palace Museum South Gate up to the Mahendra Statue.
Singha Durbar Area:
From the eastern corner of Bhadrakali Temple (Tinkune Island) east to the Singha Durbar Main Gate,
From the front of Singha Durbar Main Gate north to the turn of Panchodaya Secondary School,

From Panchodaya turn east past the Office of the Auditor General to the north-eastern turn of Singha Durbar leading to Anamnagar, and from that turn through the Singha Durbar East Gate up to Hanumansthan Temple,
From the front of Singha Durbar Main Gate south past the Supreme Court up to the Department of Archaeology intersection,
From the Department of Archaeology intersection through the Singha Durbar South Gate up to Hanumansthan Temple,
From the eastern entrance of the District Court to the northern turn of the District Administration Office.
Prime Minister's Residence Baluwatar Area:
From Baluwatar Pavitra Workshop Chowk through Nepal Rastra Bank to Italia Chowk, and via the Chinese Embassy to the stone spout (Dhungedhara) in front of Lakshmi Bank towards Bhatbhateni,
From Italia Chowk west to the Residence of the Chairperson of the National Assembly and up to 100 meters from said residence towards the road to Gairidhara,
From the north-eastern road of the Prime Minister's Residence to the Chief Justice's Residence turn and 100 meters west from that turn,
From the Chief Justice's Residence turn south-east to the turn of the National Assembly Chairperson's Residence,
Up to 100 meters on the road heading east from Rastra Bank Chowk.
Parliament House New Baneshwor Area:
